plant.description: Unique History: Sacredly known as 'The Indian Sherbet Berry'. Mentioned in ancient texts for its flower-based dyes used during the Holi festival. Historically used to symbolize the 'spiritual fire' of the seeker. Physical Characteristics: A small-to-medium-sized deciduous tree with thick, rough, greyish-brown bark. The leaves are opposite, ovate, and very rough to the touch. The fruit is an oval drupe, turning dark-purple or black when ripe. Found throughout India and Southeast Asia and cultivated globably for fruit and medicine. Specific Chemical Compounds: Contains Anthocyanins, Nyctanthin, D-mannitol, and Benzoic acid. The presence of anthocyanins is crucial for its anti-viral and anti-inflammatory action. Strict Standardizations: The fruit is standardized in API based on its dark-purple color and characteristic sweet-astringent taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<10%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>5%).

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Powerful Pitta and Vata Hara (pacifies Pitta and Vata doshas). Due to its Madhura (sweet) and Kashaya (astringent) Rasa and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Shulahara' (pain-relieving) and 'Daha-prashamana' (burning-relieving) therapies. The fruit's cooling nature calms the fiery and agitated qualities of Pitta.

fruit_juice.method: 20-50ml of fresh juice taken with sugar/honey for cooling heart health.

bark_powder.method: 1-3g taken twice daily for chronic diarrhea.

cold_infusion.method: 10g of crushed seeds soaked in 60ml water overnight; filtered for managing thirst and acidity.

fruit_pulp_paste.method: Ground fresh fruit applied directly to localized inflammations (Mumps, boils, skin rashes) to reduce heat and swelling.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 10 - 20 ml of fruit juice mixed with honey twice daily for cooling during childhood infections.
dosage.adults: Swarasa (Juice): 20-50 ml; Powder: 1-3 grams; Decoction: 20-40 ml.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related Pitta issues and maintaining cardiac health. Dosage: 10ml juice daily.

medicinal_uses: Expert Cooling and Heart Tonic Specialist (Hridya): Parushaka is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions of extreme internal heat and cardiac agitation. Its 'Sheeta' (cooling) nature provided by anthocyanins helps in providing instant relief from palpitations, stress-related high blood pressure, and inflammatory fevers. WHO research confirms its potent anti-oxidant and heart-soothing action.

Gastrointestinal and Diarrhea Care: Its 'Grahi' (absorbent) and 'Kashaya' (astringent) actions make it highly effective for managing burning hemorrhoids, chronic diarrhea, and inflammatory bowel conditions by strengthening the gut lining and binding the stool.

Vision and Eye health Support: Acts as a powerful agent for managing eye health. It helps in reducing redness, burning sensations, and discharge from the eyes in conditions like conjunctivitis (Abhishyanda). It is used to improve skin radiance and prevent age-related skin aging and discoloration.
Urinary and Kidney Care: As a mild diuretic, it helps in clearing urinary stagnations and managing infections by cooling the urinary tract and expelling toxins from the blood.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Anthocyanins (Cyanidin-3-glucoside), Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol), and Tannins. Cyanidin-3-glucoside is the primary bioactive responsible for its potent anti-inflammatory and heart-soothing action.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb and fruit with a history of use spanning millennia across various global cultures and traditional systems without documented toxicity.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Kapha (cold, excess mucus) should use it in moderation as it is Sheeta (cooling). Avoid during active extreme constipation due to its 'Grahi' (absorbent) nature.
possible_side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin cooling or stomach dryness.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-hypertensive or anticoagulant medications.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
